figures.caption: Table 1: Representative methods by compression-spectrum position. The year column shows how influential lines occupy different compression targets, from observation-faithful reconstruction to task-relevant abstraction.
Table 5: Functional evaluation matrix. P = primary design target, p = partial / side-effect, – = not targeted.
Table 6: Preserve/discard/enable companion matrix: what each latent function must retain, throw away, and unlock.
Table 2: Operational reading of the three sufficiency propositions.
Table 4: Seven evaluation axes for actionable latent states.
Table 3: Action-grounding spectrum from R0 to R4: how action enters latent dynamics across world-model families.

Figure 2: The taxonomy of latent state design. Six roles partition the world-model literature by what the latent is primarily for; three cross-cutting criteria qualify how well a latent satisfies its role across all six.
